title: Consumer (narrow hook) — read payload.entries + accept version 2 + silent-skip v1
description: Update the PreToolUse narrow-injection hook to read the renamed wire field `payload.entries` and to enforce the `version: 2` gate per the v1-receipt stance from TASK-001 (silent-skip with one-line stderr breadcrumb). NO accept-both compat. After this commit the hook ONLY understands v2 payloads.

files.target: line 627-639 (renderSummary function) + main() payload-handling site (locate via the readPayload call after line ~654)
files.change: In renderSummary (line 627-): add a leading version-gate `if (!payload || payload.version !== 2) { if (payload && payload.version !== undefined) { stderr.write('[fabric] hint payload version=' + payload.version + ' unsupported (expected 2), skipping\n'); } return []; }` (note: stderr access requires plumbing — pass stderr via a closure or reuse the existing module-level `process.stderr`). Rename `payload.narrow` reader at line 628 → `payload.entries`. Update the function-doc block (lines 616-626) to document the v2 gate. The variable name `narrow` inside renderSummary should be renamed to `entries` for consistency with the wire name; cascading rename through lines 631-637 (banner string `narrow.length` → `entries.length`, loop var).

implementation: Add a version-gate block as the FIRST statement in renderSummary: `if (!payload || payload.version !== 2) { if (payload && payload.version !== undefined) { try { process.stderr.write('[fabric] hint payload version=' + payload.version + ' unsupported (expected 2), skipping\n'); } catch {} } return []; }` — wrap the stderr write in try/catch to honor the existing silent-on-failure contract
Rename the field read at line 628: `payload.narrow` → `payload.entries`

Cascading rename of local `narrow` variable to `entries` in renderSummary (lines 628, 629, 632, 634) — this is just consistency-with-wire, the function still works either way but matching the wire name reduces cognitive load
Update the function docstring (lines 616-626) to add a line documenting the v2 protocol gate and the silent-skip-with-breadcrumb behavior on version mismatch
Verify the renamed field flows through `formatEntryLine(entry)` at line 635 — the loop variable `entry` is unchanged, only the source array changes

Manual smoke: run `node packages/cli/templates/hooks/knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs` with a stub payload `{version: 2, entries: [{id:'X', type:'decision', maturity:'proven', summary:'test'}]}` piped to stdin (or via test seam) and confirm a non-empty stderr render
Commit message: `feat(rc18)!: knowledge-hint-narrow hook reads payload.entries (protocol v2)`

reference.pattern: Mirror the v2-gate + field-rename pattern from TASK-004 (broad consumer); both consumers MUST use the identical version-gate code so any future protocol bump can be done with a single sed
reference.examples: The version-gate code block is identical to TASK-004's; copy-paste is intentional and a feature, not a smell — protocol-version checks SHOULD be uniform across consumers

criteria: File modified: packages/cli/templates/hooks/knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs at line 627-639 (renderSummary) plus the surrounding doc block

Version-gate present: `payload.version !== 2` check exists as the first statement in renderSummary (verified via `grep -A1 'function renderSummary' packages/cli/templates/hooks/knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs | grep -q 'version !== 2'`)

v1-receipt stance enforced: gate returns `[]` AND emits stderr breadcrumb when version mismatches (verified by piping a v1 payload through the hook and observing exactly one stderr line + zero stdout)

Test command: `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli vitest run knowledge-hint-narrow` — pre-TASK-005 this WILL fail because fixtures still use `version: 1` and `payload.narrow`; that failure is the desired signal the protocol is now v2-only
Behavioral expectation: with a v2 payload the hook renders the existing narrow-match block; with a v1 payload the hook emits one stderr breadcrumb line and produces no other output
Field-name consistency check: emitter (`entries`) ↔ this consumer (`payload.entries`) ↔ stderr breadcrumb cites version mismatch only (no field-name reference) — all three aligned
Version-receipt stance check: piping `{version: 1, narrow: [...]}` produces stderr `[fabric] hint payload version=1 unsupported (expected 2), skipping` and zero further output
Single git commit recorded with breaking-change marker (`!`)

rationale.chosen_approach: Apply the v2 gate uniformly at the renderSummary entry point so any code path leading to render is gated; do not gate inside main() because the hook does additional non-render work (edit-counter at line 658-665) that should run even on v1 receipt for telemetry continuity
alternatives_considered: Gate inside main() (would skip the edit-counter on version mismatch — bad for telemetry)
decision_factors: Mirrors broad consumer (TASK-004 does the same)
rationale.tradeoffs: If a future v3 needs a different gate semantic (e.g. opt-in reading), it is a renderSummary-level change again — accepted

manual_checks: Run `pnpm -F @fenglimg/fabric-cli vitest run knowledge-hint-narrow` and confirm the failures are field-name/version mismatches in fixtures (expected pre-TASK-005)
Smoke-test by piping a hand-crafted v1 payload and confirm exactly one stderr breadcrumb line, no other output
success_metrics: Single grep result for `payload.narrow` is zero (clean rename)
